page: The Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is a virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model and a single thread per core. Memory capacity is 64.0 GB, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 480 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded capabilities alongside moderate multi-threaded performance. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core stress-ng performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also register average performance. For LLM workloads, text generation using a small 135M model reaches top-tier speeds, whereas prompt processing with a medium 7B model is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound tasks without requiring dedicated compute-optimized pricing. The primary tradeoff lies in the shared CPU allocation, which may introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads, as reflected in the average multi-core benchmark tiers. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight text generation tasks that benefit from the 480 GB local NVMe SSD and 64.0 GB of system memory.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor clocked at 2.0 GHz under a shared CPU allocation model, it includes a 480 GB NVMe SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and small-model text generation, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. Prompt processing for medium-sized LLMs represents a performance bottleneck, landing in the bottom 25%. With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server provides a memory-optimized profile suitable for database hosting, memory-intensive applications, and static web serving where high memory density is required but dedicated CPU cores are not critical.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 server with 8 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 server featuring 8 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC-powered virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Suited for database hosting, static web serving, and memory-heavy applications
